details.text: this verse uniquely begins with الَّذِينَ which is an اِسْم المَوْصُول and is usually a joining and connecting word but here it starts abruptly with it at the beginning of the sentence.

Derivative: مَوْلَى. The difference between وَلِي (Wali) and مَوْلَى (Mawla). وَلِي (Wali) is someone who wants to protect you, looks out for your wellbeing and takes responsibility for you. مَوْلَى (Mawla) is someone who actively aids and protects you. The difference between protective and protecting. Disbelievers can have a Wali or Awliya’ (protective friends) but they can have no Mawla (لَا مَوْلَىٰ لَهُمْ) i.e., the One who can actually protect them and ensure their protection.
Derivative: آنِفًا means “just now”, like what did he say “just now”, or it refers to the statement or text or matter that was aforementioned or abovementioned.
Derivative: أَشْرَاطُ is the plural of شَرَطٌ and it refers to signs or portents of something to come i.e., the Day of Judgement.
Derivative: مُتَقَلَّبَ refers to changing and moving about i.e., مُتَقَلَّبَكُمْ refers to your continuous movement from one place, reality or circumstance to another.

Derivative: ذَنْبِ comes from the word ذَنَبٌ which means the tail of animal and ذَنْبِ (Dhanb) is used to refer to sins or deeds that are not necessarily evil in themselves but their consequences are bad. Deeds which are evil in themselves are سَيِّئَة (Sayyi’ah) whilst a ذَنْبِ (Dhanb) may not be bad in origin but its consequences are bad or undesirable. ذَنْبِ (Dhanb) can also imply a point of embarrassment and something that is undesirable and is referred to as such in Arabic poetry. ذَنْبِ (Dhanb) in this context in respect to the Prophet ﷺ can be viewed as a strategic error that can only be called out as such by the Divine.
Derivative: مَثْوَى is the place that you stay, reside and settle in.
Derivative: بَالَ – Refers here to a “state” and “condition”.
- The مَا in بِمَا is an expression of generality. As such, here it means “whatever” was revealed upon Muhammad ﷺ which encompasses all revelation, including the Qur’an and the Sunnah.

- أَوْلَىٰ (Awla) for or to them (لَهُمْ) is an expression that means a curse and threat of punishment against them. Another possible meaning is that it is means: “It is more fitting for them” i.e., that they just pass out in death.
Derivative: تَوَلَّيْتُمْ from تَوَلَّى and it can mean to turn away from something and it can also mean to assume control or command over something.

Derivative: يَتَدَبَّرُونَ from the verb تَدَبَّرَ which has origins in دُبُرٌ which means a back of something. It means that when you hear or see something you wonder what lies behind it and you don’t just think about what is upon the surface of something. تَدَبَّرَ therefore means to think and contemplate deeply about something and to examine something thoroughly i.e., from top to bottom, from the back to the front.
Derivative: سَوَّلَ – تَسْوِيل means to make something easy, light and no big deal through seduction or enticement i.e., Shaytan beautifies something ugly for you.
Derivative: أَمْلَىٰ - إِمْلاء means to fill with worldly long-term hopes.
Derivative: إِسْرَارَ from أَسَرَّ refers to something that you try hard to keep secret and differs from سِرٌّ which merely means a secret.
Derivative: أَضْغَانَ is the plural of ضَغَنٌ and it refers to a hatred and grudge that is harboured and held within someone towards something.
Derivative: يَتِرَ from وَتَرَ and it means to deprive you of what you are due. Here it means in terms of the reward for your deeds.

وَلَا يَسْأَلْكُمْ أَمْوَالَكُمْ – the fact that this is جَواب الشَّرْط (the response based on conditionality) connected to the إِنْ (if), provides a possible different understanding to what many have interpreted this to mean. it would mean that: “if you believe and have taqwa, allah will then give you your recompense and then he will not ask you for your wealth i.e., in the hereafter” instead of it being interpreted to mean that allah will not ask us you for your money or all of it in this life.
Derivative: يُحْفِ means to press, put strain and pressure upon i.e., it would be difficult and a hardship.
Derivative: أَثْخَنتُمُوهُمْ from أَثْخَنَ – إِثْخان. It means to overcome, defeat and inflict many wounds upon them and shed a lot of the enemy’s blood.
- “If you help or support Allah” means: If you support His Messenger ﷺ and His Deen, and struggle and exert yourself in His cause.
Derivative: تَعْسًا means to perish and to taste misery and destitution.
Derivative: أَحْبَطَ – إِحْباط means to cancel out and nullify something and to make it worthless.

Derivative: أَعْمَالَ when used in the Qur'an general refers to deeds which are good or have merit in origin. Therefore, it implies here that even those deeds which in themselves had merit will be rendered worthless (فَأَحْبَطَ أَعْمَالَهُمْ) because of their hatred of Allah’s revelation.
